title: Your First Rust App
description: Write your very first Hello World program in Rust.

slides.title: The Main Event 🎬

quizData.question: Which keyword is used to declare a function in Rust?
quizData.explanation: In Rust, the `fn` keyword is short for function.

slides.title: Printing to the Screen 🖨️
Macros vs Functions 🪄
slides.content:
In Rust, anytime you see a `!` after a name, it means you are calling a **macro**, not a normal function.

Macros are code that write other code! For now, just remember: `println!` needs an exclamation mark!

quizData.question: What does the `!` in `println!` indicate?

options.text: It means the code is important
It means we are calling a macro, not a function

quizData.explanation: The `!` indicates a macro in Rust. Macros are more flexible than functions.

slides.title: Semicolons Rule ;
slides.content:
# Don't forget the `;`

In Rust, almost every line of code inside a function must end with a semicolon `;`.

This tells the compiler where one statement ends and the next begins.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Run your very first complete Rust program!
